'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Kobe|Chuo Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.
This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”
== Overview == As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] began, and a cannon from that era.
In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] or the Bizen Incident.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe) Front.jpg|Full view of the front of Sannomiya Jinja File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe).JPG|The shrine’s torii gate and commemorative stone </gallery> {{clear}}

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Kobe|Chuo Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.
This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”
== Overview == As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] began, and a cannon from that era.
In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] or the Bizen Incident.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe) Front.jpg|Full view of the front of Sannomiya Jinja File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe).JPG|The shrine’s torii gate and commemorative stone </gallery> {{clear}}
== Access == * [[:en:Kyūkyoryūchi-Daimarumae Station|Kyukyoryuchi-Daimarumae Station]] – [[:en:Kaigan Line|Kaigan Line]] * [[:en:Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)]] ** [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] ** [[:en:Hanshin Electric Railway|Hanshin]] [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Main line]]・[[:en:Tōzai Line (Kobe)|Kobe Kosoku Line]]

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Kobe|Chuo Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.
This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”
== Overview == As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] began, and a cannon from that era.
In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] or the Bizen Incident.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe) Front.jpg|Full view of the front of Sannomiya Jinja File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe).JPG|The shrine’s torii gate and commemorative stone </gallery> {{clear}}

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Kobe|Chuo Ward]], [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.
This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”
== Overview == As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] began, and a cannon from that era.
In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the [[:en:Kobe Incident|Kobe Incident]] or the Bizen Incident.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe) Front.jpg|Full view of the front of Sannomiya Jinja File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe).JPG|The shrine’s torii gate and commemorative stone </gallery> {{clear}}

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|WD=Q686802|lt=Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine}}. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.

This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”

== Overview ==
As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident}} began, and a cannon from that era.

In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident}} or the Bizen Incident.

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|WD=Q686802|lt=Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine}}. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.

This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”

== Overview ==
As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident}} began, and a cannon from that era.

In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident}} or the Bizen Incident.

== Gallery ==
<gallery>
File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe) Front.jpg|Full view of the front of Sannomiya Jinja
File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe).JPG|The shrine’s torii gate and commemorative stone
</gallery>
{{clear}}

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|WD=Q686802|lt=Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine}}. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.

This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”

== Overview ==
As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident}} began, and a cannon from that era.

In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident}} or the Bizen Incident.

{{clear}}

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|WD=Q686802|lt=Chuo Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine|1=Ikuta Shrine}}. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.

This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”

== Overview ==
As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident|1=Kobe Incident}} began, and a cannon from that era.

In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident|1=Kobe Incident}} or the Bizen Incident.

'''Sannomiya Jinja''' (三宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|de|Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|ja|三宮町 (神戸市)|lt=Sannomiya-chō|lt_ja=三宮町|lt_de=Sannomiya-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya-chō (Kobe City)|1=Sannomiya-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355149}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|WD=Q686802|lt=Chuo Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the third among the eight shrines collectively known as the {{ill|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|de|Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|ja|生田裔神八社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta Eight Sub-shrines|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574819}}, each of which enshrines one of the offspring deities of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine|1=Ikuta Shrine}}. The chief deity of this shrine is {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|de|Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}.

This shrine also gave its name to the surrounding area known as “Sannomiya.”

== Overview ==
As a guardian of safe navigation and the prosperity of commerce and industry, this shrine has long been deeply revered by the general populace. Because no ancient records remain, the exact date of its founding is unknown. Its enshrined deity, Takitsuhime-no-Mikoto, is a goddess of water. Within the precincts stand the “Kawara Reisha,” a monument marking the historical site where the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident|1=Kobe Incident}} began, and a cannon from that era.

In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1st year of the Meiji era, 1868), a conflict broke out in front of Sannomiya Jinja between soldiers of the Okayama Domain (Bizen Domain) and foreign troops. This became the first diplomatic incident for the Meiji government, referred to as the {{ill|Kobe Incident|en|Kobe Incident|WD=Q11589796|lt=Kobe Incident|1=Kobe Incident}} or the Bizen Incident.
